item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Much too wordy for my taste. I generally don't like the IJAMEA skips because they are extremely bloated and tedious to read. Even if the anomaly itself is interesting, they go on and on until you lose all desire to find out what's going on.

I would prune it heavily, removing one of the excerpts would be a start. Until then, I'll have to downvote.

item.title: Please don’t hate me
item.content:encoded: At the risk of sounding immature, I must declare I gave the 69th upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On one hand, a neat reference to Japanese culture/myth. On the other, this article was initially shown to me by a furry (for reasons that I think should be obvious). I'd say it isn't fetishy inherently… but the skip DOES describe "growth of extra teats (up to four per breast) on the breast", which implies multi-tit boobs (if I am reading this correctly), and that's… definitely fetishy. Correct me if I misunderstood.

I like the historical sci-fan stuff, though, so I'll go with a no-vote on this one.

+0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, it is good to highlight concerns and I will sate your curiosity at least.

Transfigurations are more prominent and occur at an increased rate in female subjects than male subjects.

Since the original entity is a female, a female body would be more receptacle to the transformation than a male.

and the extra breast growth/extra teats.

Red foxes tend to have four pairs of them, and it references SCP-953 (which has been compared to a red fox). 'Teat' was used since it connotes animals, as compared to 'nipple' (usually implying human).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually liked the description of Tamamo-no-Mae. It's really nice, classical imagery, and it works with the character, plus you're right that it would be strange to magically give her clothes :P

What I was referring to specifically was

Transfigurations are more prominent and occur at an increased rate in female subjects than male subjects.

and the extra breast growth/extra teats. (I do agree with your use of "breasts" rather than "chest.") Not a huge criticism, it just broke immersion for me for a little bit before it became clear over the next sentence or two that this wasn't another 597. I guess if I had a suggestion it would be maybe to move the sentences about breast growth from the end of the bulletproof list to somewhere near the beginning/middle to clarify that it's just part of the transformation. But it really doesn't matter too much, I was just curious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
could you explain a little more why the transformation happens the way it does?

TL;DR, the rock is processed from a fox spirit and contact with it changes one's appearance to look like a fox spirit (commonly portrayed in Chinese-Japanese-Korean myths as a human-fox hybrid).

The prominence of female subjects and the focus on the breasts make it read as fetishy to me

I will counter your claim of "female subjects". On the Foundation's side, no test subject is shown here. On IJAMEA's side, I have this…

Today, we proceed to test the effect on a live human should a small portion of Sessho-seki be extracted. As expected, today's volunteer responded positively upon contact with Sessho-seki. As with yesterday's volunteer, he will be given relief off his earthly tethers for his troubles.

Although, given that Japanese language usually don't use gendered third person pronoun, it is likely to be undefined.

But of course, you might be referring to Tamamo-no-Mae with this description:

She was in human form partially, with her nine red tails wrapping her otherwise nude body.

Well… I am only going to put readers further into the suspense of disbelief if I made her manifest clothes magically. It is within possibility that IJAMEA to give her clothes. But I woudl regard her nakedness to be an allusion to the fox spirit's traditional role as a seductress (a role Tamamo-no-Mae performed on her myth).

And onto your second clam on "breast", I would like to remind you that 'breast' is an organ of the human body. While it does attract a certain sexual connotation, it is the official scientific term for the organ (and Wikipedia gave me nothing else to work with). To say 'chest' is too broad and barely defines anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sadly, by about halfway through I was lost and didn't finish the SCP, so I haven't voted. Not a criticism - I have mental issues that make it hard for me to concentrate on a lot of SCPs regardless of quality - just a preface to my question.

I just wanted to ask, could you explain a little more why the transformation happens the way it does? The prominence of female subjects and the focus on the breasts make it read as fetishy to me, almost so much so that I'd suggest revision, but with how well the rest is researched and put together it seems like there's a reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Smashed the upvote button when I realised this skip was about Tamamo, and now that I have finished reading it I wish I could upvote again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting write-up, but I think I've got some biases that are preventing me from giving a fair assessment of the article. Mostly that, after watching Soul Hunter, seeing the use of a fox demon for war being called "Operation Dakki" hits a little too on-the-nose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely bloody brilliant, as always, MrWrong. You write the absolute best historical science-fantasy skips, and you keep bringing in things that not only make a ton of sense, but have mostly been unexplored on this site so far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mind you that 953 has psionic abilities and all we get was the writings of someone who interacted with the fox spirit. He thinks she cooperated, but did she really? Whether Tamamo is 953 or not, it definitely tweaked the IJAMEA operatives' minds for its purpose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doubting that Tamamo-no-Mae is really that skip since it was cooperative with the Japanese and didn't torture them for its amusement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thoroughly enjoyed this. Good use of traditional folklore.

I would have upvoted just for General Nura of the Youkai Battalion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Abe is a somewhat common surname in Japan. It's kinda a reference to him, since it was his descendant who exposed Tamamo in the legend.

I honestly won't think Abe no Seimei would be that dumb to fall into a fox spirit's trap (he is said to be half-fox after all).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Quick question:
Is Dr. Abe supposed to be an immortal Abe no Seimei, or is the name just a coincidence?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have a real talent for writing these sorts of skips, and I hope to see more in the future. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not gonna lie, when I started reading the transfiguration description I thought this was going to be some weird elaborate furry fetish thing. But when I got to the end, it was a pretty solid article that made some good references to Asian cultures and customs.
+1

item.title: Thanks, Attribution and Other Matters
item.content:encoded:
This article was largely inspired from a few words from Scorpion451 and Steeltitan, which inspired me to do this up. Also, thank you to the aforementioned two and others such as Solo, Aiden Eldritch etc. for looking this through.

Attributions are:

IJAMEA was from SCP-2937 by Mazyrian
"Semitic solar deity" was from this article in the GOC canon, compiled by Clef
SCP-953 (Polymorphic Humanoid) by Clef

And while it is cool to make shapeshifting bullets, they are meaningless before good old-fashion military-grade weapons.


So this implies that 953 may either be Tamamo-no-Mae that the IJAMEA have awakened OR a random kumiho caught by them and it played along the IJAMEA's misconceptions. It does give insight on why 953 is so malicious to people in general and that she is beyond a generic monster.

If you wish to, you may read between the paragraphs…
